criteria: The builder renders an existing definition as a downward tree with branches fanning sideways + rounded orthogonal connectors; no diagonal/upward lines; layout is auto-computed (no manual drag). Verified in a REAL browser (Playwright).

A user can assemble trigger → wait → send → exit AND an if-branch (yes/no each ending in a send→exit), save it, reload, and get the same graph back (round-trips through the DSL). Saving an invalid graph is refused.
The editor cannot create a loop, a back-edge, or an orphan node; deleting a node re-links to a valid tree. Workspace-scoped; data-testid contract preserved + extended.
phase.refs: CLAUDE.md (UI kit, BroadcastComposer/SegmentBuilder patterns, data-testid e2e contract) + the locked canvas design
scope: Replace the placeholder AutomationBuilder.tsx with a constrained DOWNWARD CANVAS that RENDERS a AutomationDefinition: auto-layout (each node below its parent; condition/multi-way branches fan to the SIDES and re-pack to avoid overlap; positions COMPUTED, not stored), rounded ORTHOGONAL connectors (no diagonals, no up/back arrows), pan/scroll. A node shows its type + summary; an insertion control (+) on each edge inserts a step; nodes are deletable (re-linking the graph so it stays a valid down-only tree with no orphans).

Maintain the no-loop / no-orphan / single-trigger invariants in the editor (you can never create a back-edge or an unconnected node); build/parse between the canvas model and the DSL {startNode, nodes}; save via POST/PUT /automations (definition) — reject an invalid graph (validateAutomationDefinition) with a clear message.

Node-type palette for inserting: wait, wait-until, hour-of-day window, if/branch, send-email, update-profile (set_attribute), webhook, exit (editors come in phase 6 — here, inserting creates a node with sensible defaults / a stub config). Keep it on the workspace design system; stable data-testid on every interactive element.
phase.title: Builder: constrained downward canvas (auto-layout + connectors)

instructions: Run `git add -A`. If not on a feature branch and on the default branch, create/use a branch first.
Commit with a clear conventional message describing the phase (Builder: constrained downward canvas (auto-layout + connectors)), gate score (95), converged=true.
End the commit message body with: Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Do NOT push. Return the commit hash. Return ONLY the JSON.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { committed: boolean, commitHash: string, message: string }
prompt.task: Stage and git-commit the work for "Builder: constrained downward canvas (auto-layout + connectors)".
